title: WhatsApp → Safety Observation
subtitle: Demo 2 · site screenshots, integrated to Forma

inputs.label: WhatsApp Screenshots
inputs.description: Screenshots from a site WhatsApp group — containing chat messages, on-site photos, and informal hazard discussions captured by the team.
inputs.inputFormat: Screenshot images — a mix of chat messages and on-site photos.

persona.quote: The team flags hazards in our site chat group — I screenshot the thread and turn it into a proper safety record before anything gets missed.

stations.label: CAPTURE
stations.description: Reads the screenshots and separates the conversation text from the embedded site photos.

agents.name: Conversation Summary
agents.description: Reads the screenshots and produces a clear summary of the reported hazard — covering what happened, where, and who was involved.
agents.output: Hazard summary: issue, location, time, and people involved.

agents.name: Image Analysis
agents.description: Scans the on-site photos to spot unsafe conditions, hazard objects, and relevant scene details.
agents.output: Hazard objects and unsafe conditions identified from photos.

stations.label: UNDERSTAND
stations.description: Retrieves the official hazard taxonomy and assigns the correct category to the identified hazard.

agents.name: SGBuildex
agents.description: Queries the SGBuildex API to retrieve the official Singapore hazard categories and safety observation requirements for this project.
agents.output: Official hazard categories and required fields confirmed.

agents.name: Hazard Identification
agents.description: Matches each detected hazard to the correct official category and severity level from the Singapore reference database.
agents.output: Hazard classified: official category and severity confirmed.

stations.label: STRUCTURE
stations.description: Creates the safety observation form in Autodesk and attaches the photo evidence.

agents.name: Form
agents.description: Fills in the Autodesk safety observation form using the hazard summary and classification. The completed form is ready for review or direct submission.
agents.output: Safety observation form completed in Autodesk.

agents.name: Relationship
agents.description: Attaches the site photo to the safety record as evidence, keeping the observation traceable back to what was seen on the ground.
agents.output: Site photo attached to the observation as evidence.

stations.label: GOVERN
stations.description: Triggers the data pipeline to refresh the management Power BI dashboard with the latest safety statistics.

agents.name: Dashboard
agents.description: Triggers the downstream data pipeline to refresh the Power BI dashboard, ensuring management sees up-to-date safety statistics within minutes.
agents.output: Management dashboard updated with the latest safety statistics.

outputs.label: Automated Form Workflow
outputs.description: A classified safety observation with photo evidence — created in Autodesk Forma automatically.

outputs.label: Real-Time Insight
outputs.description: Management sees up-to-date statistics on Power BI for hazards being reported.

steps.caption: A site safety engineer screenshots a hazard discussion from the team's WhatsApp group.
Screenshots arrive — a mix of chat messages and on-site photos of a reported hazard.
The Conversation Summary Agent reads the thread; Image Analysis scans the photos for hazard objects.
SGBuildex retrieves the official hazard taxonomy; Hazard Identification assigns the correct category.
The Form Agent creates the Autodesk observation record; Relationship attaches the photo as evidence.
The Dashboard Agent triggers the data pipeline — management's Power BI view updates in real time.
A classified, evidence-linked safety observation is created in Autodesk Forma.
The Power BI dashboard refreshes — site safety statistics are current for management.
